<trait label="Edifice" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=445" />
</traits>

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 544

An academy gives your citizens—and the PCs themselves— an institution where advanced study in many fields can be pursued, researched, and referenced.

---

<row gap="medium">
**Lots** 2

**Cost** 52 RP, 12 Lumber, 6 Luxuries, 12 Stone
</row>

**Upgrade To**

---

</row>

[Industry](/Skills.aspx?ID=25) (trained) DC 16

+1 item bonus to [Demolish](/Actions.aspx?ID=1391)

**Effects**
Treat the settlement's level as one level higher than its actual level for the purposes of determining which [alchemical items](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6) are readily available for sale in that settlement. This effect stacks up to three times. Checks attempted to [Identify Alchemy](/Actions.aspx?ID=44) in any settlement with at least one alchemy laboratory gain a +1 item bonus.

</column>

<title level="1" right="Kingdom Structure 2" pfs="">[Bridge](/KMStructures.aspx?ID=8)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Infrastructure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=444" />
</traits>

**Source** [Kingmaker Adventure Path](/Sources.aspx?ID=150) pg. 545

Bridges give settlements that have water borders a connection to land (but at the GM's option, a border on a lake might not be able to use bridges).

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**Lots** —

**Cost** 6 RP, 1 Lumber or 1 Stone
</row>

**Construction**
[Engineering](/Skills.aspx?ID=22) DC 16

**Effects**
A bridge allows an island settlement to provide influence (see [Influence](/Rules.aspx?ID=1830)), negates the Trade penalty for island settlements (see [Land Borders](/Rules.aspx?ID=1821)), and allows travel over its associated Water Border with ease (see [Navigating an Urban Grid](/Rules.aspx?ID=1822)). Bridges can only be built on Water Borders. When you build a bridge, check the “Bridge” box on one of the Water Borders on your Urban Grid to indicate its location.

</column>
name: Bridge

A cathedral serves as a focal point of spiritual worship in the settlement and the seat of regional power for a religion. Most cathedrals are astounding works of art and eye-catching marvels of architecture.

**Effects**
The first time you build a cathedral in a turn, reduce Unrest by 4. While in a settlement with a cathedral, you gain a +3 item bonus to [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) and [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks made to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) while [Investigating](/Actions.aspx?ID=516), and to all faith-themed checks made while [Researching](/Rules.aspx?ID=1205). Only creatures with Intelligence modifiers of –4 or –5 are appropriate to place in a menagerie. A kingdom gains 1 Unrest at the start of a Kingdom turn for each sapient creature (anything with an Intelligence modifier of –3 or higher) on display in a menagerie.
